examples: He said, "I think thou callest thyself Miles Hendon, if I heard thee aright?"
The physician addressed bowed low, and replied, "It jumpeth with my own conviction, sire, that thou hast divined aright."
Teach us to number our days aright, that we may gain a heart of wisdom.
There's also a motion under Section 4A (2) of the Parliamentary Standards Act, which (if I read it aright) concerns the Commons power to punish MPs by withholding salaries.
All crises, viewed aright, are opportunities.
